description_long: This church features a striking facade with two tall, pointed spires flanking a central entrance, above which sits a large circular rose window. The exterior walls are primarily a light stone color, adorned with vertical, narrow arched windows and subtle decorative elements. The roof is steeply pitched and covered in vibrant red tiles, with multiple gables and a complex, multi-sectioned layout. The side elevations reveal a series of tall, lancet windows and buttresses, characteristic of Gothic Revival architecture. The rear of the church is polygonal, with additional arched windows and a continuation of the red-tiled roof. The overall structure is grand and imposing, designed to be a prominent landmark in an urban setting.
description: A grand church with twin pointed spires, a large rose window, and a steep red-tiled roof, featuring tall arched windows and detailed stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin spires, central rose window, and main entrance with detailed stonework.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long nave, red-tiled roof, and series of tall arched windows along the wall.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the polygonal apse, continuation of the red roof, and multiple arched windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar features to view 1, including the spire, nave, and arched windows.
